summary: This content explains what title insurance is in general terms and why it may be part of a home purchase transaction. It is education-only and escalates questions about personal loan terms or pricing to a licensed Loan Officer.

key_steps.title: Understand what title insurance is
key_steps.instruction: Title insurance is generally intended to help address certain title-related risks associated with property ownership, depending on policy terms.

key_steps.title: Know what a title search is
key_steps.instruction: A title search typically reviews public records related to the property. The exact process can vary by closing agent or title company.

key_steps.title: Review documents you receive
key_steps.instruction: If you receive title-related documentation, review it and ask questions if any terms or coverage details are unclear.

faq_items.question: Is title insurance the same as homeowners insurance?
faq_items.answer: They are different. Homeowners insurance generally relates to property damage and liability, while title insurance generally relates to title issues based on the policy terms.

faq_items.question: Who provides title insurance?
faq_items.answer: Title insurance is typically issued by a title company or insurer. Your closing agent can provide details on the documents you receive.

escalation_triggers.trigger_text: Which loan option should I choose for the lowest costs?
Can you calculate what I will pay each month?
